elevator_pitch: S'Mos is a nostalgia-driven social lounge built around communal fire pits, craft s'mores, and cocktails. It gives city dwellers the warmth of a campfire gathering without leaving town, and it doubles as a device-free zone where people actually talk to each other again.
problem: Urban adults crave genuine social experiences but end up at the same noisy bars and overpriced restaurants. The campfire, one of humanity's oldest gathering rituals, is completely absent from city life. Meanwhile, nostalgia for simpler times is at an all-time high, yet no one has built a real business around it.
solution: S'Mos creates an intimate, fire-pit-centered social venue where guests customize gourmet s'mores with premium chocolates, artisan marshmallows, and creative toppings. Paired with craft cocktails, hot chocolate, and a strict no-devices policy, it delivers a one-of-a-kind experience that people remember and return to.
target_market: Millennials and Gen Z in major cities looking for unique social experiences. Corporate teams seeking memorable offsite gatherings. Date-night couples wanting something beyond dinner and drinks. Families in urban areas who miss the outdoor campfire experience.
revenue_model: Per-seat cover charge ($15-25) that includes a base s'mores kit. Premium topping add-ons and signature cocktails drive high-margin upsells. Private fire pit reservations for groups at $150-300 per session. Corporate event packages and catering partnerships provide B2B revenue. Branded merchandise (marshmallow kits, mugs, candles) sold on-site and online.
key_features: Build-your-own gourmet s'mores bar with 30+ premium toppings and seasonal specials
Device-free zone policy that creates genuine human connection around the fire
Signature craft cocktails and boozy hot chocolates designed for fireside sipping
Private fire pit reservations for date nights, birthdays, and corporate team events
competitive_advantage: S'Mos is not a restaurant that happens to serve s'mores. It is an experience brand built on nostalgia, sensory design, and social connection. The device-free policy is a marketing hook that generates word-of-mouth. Low food costs on marshmallows, chocolate, and graham crackers paired with high-margin drinks create strong unit economics. The concept is highly Instagrammable (before you put your phone away), driving organic social growth.
market_size_estimate: The U.S. experiential dining market is valued at over $77 billion and growing 10%+ annually. Themed bars and experiential venues (axe throwing, escape rooms, paint-and-sip) have proven consumers will pay premiums for unique outings. Even one successful location grossing $1.5M-$2.5M annually validates the model for franchising.
go_to_market: Open the first location in a high-foot-traffic urban neighborhood (think Austin, Nashville, or Denver). Build buzz with a pop-up series before committing to a permanent space. Partner with local influencers and corporate event planners from day one. Use the device-free angle as PR bait to land earned media coverage.
risks: Seasonal demand skew toward fall and winter could limit warm-weather revenue without creative programming
Novelty factor may fade if the core experience does not evolve with rotating menus and themed events
Fire safety regulations and indoor ventilation requirements add complexity and cost to buildout
year_one_milestones: Open flagship location and achieve $120K+ monthly revenue within 6 months of launch
Host 200+ corporate and private events in the first year
Build a waitlist of 5,000+ for second location and begin franchise model exploration
startup_cost_range: $250,000 - $500,000 for first location buildout, equipment, licensing, and initial marketing
